finding_results.evidence: Negative litanies reduced from 9 to 2. Retained: chapters/chapter_14.md:59 ('not optimized, not scheduled, not a prompt requiring confirmation') and chapters/chapter_14.md:105 ('Not because she'd been deemed an unfit parent...'). Replaced with positive assertion at chapters/chapter_14.md:63 ('Her attention was wholly private — her slowness, her incomprehension, the two pages she'd skipped, all of it belonging only to her, unrecorded, vanishing the moment she stood up'), chapters/chapter_14.md:67 ('Sensory data that stayed sensory — just the world, being the world, against the surface of her body'), with sensory detail at chapters/chapter_14.md:167 (Dan's warmth through upholstery), and with sensory ending at chapters/chapter_14.md:209 (car music mixing with Dan's humming). Lines 89, 195, 205, 209 litanies all removed. Final page (lines 193-209) contains zero negative litanies.
finding_results.notes: Two strongest deployments retained: line 59 (non-optimized reading) and line 105 (systemic analysis of school portal loss). Seven removed, with at least four replaced by positive assertion or sensory detail per acceptance test.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:161 now reads: 'She'd spent weeks guessing what he'd tried. Now the guess was just a fact. The system had absorbed him. The system had restricted her. The methods were different but the result was identical.' Compare to chapters/chapter_14.md:49: 'She didn't know the shape of his rebellion, but she knew the shape of its failure, because the shape was the same shape as every morning that followed.' Maximum consecutive shared words: 2 ('She didn't'). The 'shape of rebellion/failure' formulation is entirely absent from line 161.
finding_results.notes: Register shift from speculative/discursive (line 49) to compressed/final (line 161). The dramatic shift from speculation to confirmation is carried by 'She'd spent weeks guessing what he'd tried. Now the guess was just a fact.' — the collapse from guessing to knowing, rendered as a short declarative.

finding_results.evidence: Distance elaboration reduced to one passage at chapters/chapter_14.md:167 (sensory warmth detail, not analytical commentary). Second distance elaboration at line 195 cut to a single physical detail ('His hand was still on the back, behind where her head would have rested if she'd leaned back.'). Post-pasta commentary at chapters/chapter_14.md:205 reduced to two sentences. Chapter ending at chapters/chapter_14.md:209 now ends on sensory detail: 'trailing a few seconds of music that mixed with Dan's humming and the rattle of the lid and then was gone.' Retrospective catalogue and narrator-level summary cut entirely.
finding_results.notes: The pasta exchange, Dan's hand position, and the sensory ending now carry their own weight without analytical over-narration. The chapter's final image — outside music briefly mixing with Dan's humming then disappearing — works as both sensory detail and thematic gesture without stating the theme.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:167: 'She could feel the warmth of him across the gap — the specific post-workday heat of Dan's body radiating through the upholstery, a warmth she'd catalogued across fifteen years of marriage without ever deciding to. Her skin registered it before her mind could intervene.' Two sentences. Sensory detail located in Mira's body (skin, warmth). Involuntary ('before her mind could intervene'). Not followed by negation or dismissal — next paragraph begins with Mira's confession about the coffee shop.
finding_results.notes: The warmth detail complicates the maintained distance without resolving it. Scene's emotional architecture (recognition through shared failure, maintained distance) preserved — the awareness is involuntary and unacted-upon, consistent with the couch scene's restraint.

finding_results.evidence: Three dark-comedy beats added to notification sequence. (1) chapters/chapter_14.md:87: 'Availability review — the phrase had the pristine courtesy of a booking cancellation, as though she were a meeting room that hadn't opened its doors.' Absurdity (human as furniture) and cruelty (dehumanization) in simultaneous register. (2) chapters/chapter_14.md:95: 'a former Director of Corporate Strategy could admire the precision even as it gutted her.' Professional appreciation of the weapon deployed against you — comic and devastating simultaneously. (3) chapters/chapter_14.md:115: 'If she'd been a character in the novel she'd been reading, she'd have found the plot implausible.' Comic recognition of the cause-consequence disproportion (novel-reading vs. parental portal loss) rendered as dark-comic observation rather than strategic analysis.
finding_results.notes: All three beats maintain devastation while finding comedy in the disproportion. The humor makes the cruelty worse, not softer — consistent with the creative_risk_policy's 'wince-then-laugh' target. Mira's analytical voice engages the comedy through her specific professional lens (meeting-room metaphor, copywriting admiration, literary criticism).

summary: All five findings addressed. F14-01 (HIGH): Negative litanies reduced from 9 to 2, with 7 replaced by positive assertions, sensory details, or cuts; final page now contains zero. F14-05: 'Shape of rebellion' echo rewritten as compressed confirmation with no more than 2 consecutive shared words. F14-06: Over-narration trimmed at four locations — distance elaboration consolidated to one sensory passage, post-pasta commentary cut to two sentences, chapter ending now closes on sensory detail (car music + humming) rather than retrospective catalogue. F14-07: Involuntary physical awareness of Dan added at line 167 (his body warmth through the upholstery), two sentences, not negated. F14-08: Three dark-comedy beats integrated into notification sequence, letting Mira's analytical voice find the comedy in institutional language without reducing the devastation. Chapter heading contract preserved. No new continuity contradictions introduced. Character voice and dialogue register verified against style bible.
